channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Top aide of impeached South Korean president pleads for investigators to halt detention efforts
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The top aide of impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol pleaded with law enforcement agencies on Tuesday to abandon their efforts to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month, as authorities prepared a second attempt to take him into custody.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC chief prosecutor wants Israeli objections over Netanyahu warrant to be rejected
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has told judges that Israeli objections to the investigation into the 13-month war in Gaza should be rejected.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a Gaza ceasefire and the release of hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday. Mediator Qatar said the negotiations were at the “closest point” yet to sealing a deal.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN migration agency says gang violence in Haiti leads to tripling of internally displaced people
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. migration agency says internal displacement in Haiti, largely caused by gang violence, has tripled over the last year and now surpasses 1 million people — a record in the Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Structure discovered in Jerusalem's City of David dates back to the First Temple, study finds
item.description: Archaeologists excavating a site since 2010 uncovered an eight-room structure dating back to the First Temple in 8th century BCE, that included a wine press and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 18 in Gaza, medics say, as Hamas accepts a ceasefire draft
item.description: Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed at least 18 people overnight, including women and children, health officials said Tuesday, as Israel and Hamas appeared to be narrowing in on a ceasefire deal to end the 15-month war and release dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Millions of pilgrims and naked, ash-smeared ascetics take holy dips in India's mega Hindu festival
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Tens of thousands of naked Hindu ascetics and millions of pilgrims took dips in freezing water at the confluence of sacred rivers in northern India on Tuesday, in the first of a series of major baths in the Maha Kumbh festival, the largest religious congregation on Earth.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rescuers bring out survivors and dead from a South African mine as hundreds remain trapped
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — Rescuers sent a cage-like structure down into one of South Africa’s deepest gold mines on Tuesday to bring out survivors among hundreds of illegal miners trapped for months in an abandoned shaft. More than 100 are believed to have died of starvation or dehydration.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ugandan military court rules it can try opposition figure on treachery charge carrying death penalty
item.description: A prominent opposition figure in Uganda will stand trial on the serious charge of treachery, a military court ruled Tuesday, escalating the legal trouble Kizza Besigye faces ahead of presidential elections scheduled for 2026.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European defense heavyweights say meeting Trump's military spending target won't be easy
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The defense ministers of Europe's five top military spenders said Monday they want to continue increasing their investments in defense but described meeting President-elect Donald Trump's challenge for them to raise spending to 5% of their overall economic output as complicated
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Gaza ceasefire talks press on in Qatar
item.description: Israel and Hamas appear to be inching closer to a phased ceasefire agreement, officials said Monday. However, a deal hasn't been reached yet and there are a number of obstacles that could still scupper the talks in Qatar. Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza began on Oct.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court papers say ex-NBA player Jontay Porter laid out betting scheme in a text; 6th person arrested
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A sixth person was charged Monday in the federal sports betting case involving ex-NBA player Jontay Porter, and authorities disclosed a text message Porter allegedly sent explaining how to cash in on his plans to bench himself in a January 2024 game.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Watch: White House holds press briefing as latest Israel-Hamas ceasefire emerges
item.description: The White House held a press briefing on Monday in the midst of another ceasefire deal emerging between Israel and Hamas as the war in Gaza rages on. Several sticking points remain as officials work to secure an end to more than one year of fighting in the coastal enclave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Former ambassador to Denmark: NATO would be compelled to respond to Greenland invasion
item.description: Rufus Gifford, a former U.S. ambassador to Denmark, said in a Sunday interview that the NATO alliance would be compelled to respond to any invasion or incursion into Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden calls for immediate cease-fire in call with Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a phone call on Sunday to discuss the ongoing ceasefire and hostage release negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's exports in December up 10.7%, beating estimates as higher US tariffs loom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — China’s exports in December grew at a faster pace than expected, as factories rushed to fill orders to beat higher tariffs that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has threatened to impose once he takes office.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: India kicks off a massive Hindu festival touted as the world's largest religious gathering
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Millions of Hindu devotees, mystics and holy men and women from all across India flocked to the northern city of Prayagraj on Monday to kickstart the Maha Kumbh festival, which is being touted as the world's largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo says several towns recaptured from rebels but fighting intensifies in its east
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Several towns that fell to armed groups in eastern Congo’s North Kivu and South Kivu provinces have been recaptured by government forces, a Congolese army spokesman said, even as the rebels have made advances in other areas amid intensified fighting in the conflict-battered region
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KATUMWA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Ukraine has offered assistance on California wildfires
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that his country has offered assistance on the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine has captured 2 North Korean soldiers, South Korea's intelligence service says
item.description: Ukraine captured two wounded North Korean soldiers who were fighting on behalf of Russia in a Russian border region, South Korea’s intelligence service said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspended South Korean President Yoon to skip first day of impeachment trial over safety concerns
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol will not attend the first day of his impeachment trial this week, according to his lawyer, who cited concerns for the suspended president’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9
